plan.title: Restructure app.py — per-agent cl.Step visibility in Chainlit

context.project: py-agno-ai-workflow — AI pipeline mapping free-text job categories to O*NET canonical titles
context.stack: Python 3.12, Agno ≥ 1.0, Chainlit, uv, Pydantic v2, rapidfuzz, DuckDB, openpyxl
context.entry_point: app.py — run with: chainlit run app.py
context.agent_pipeline: IngestAgent → ValidatorAgent → MapperAgent → AuditWriter (linear, deterministic order)

rules.rule: Never instantiate LLM directly in agent file — always import from infrastructure/llm/provider.py
All agent output uses output_schema + Pydantic v2 — never free-text strings
rapidfuzz runs before every LLM call — mandatory pre-filter
Before writing code that calls any library in this stack, look up the current API with Context7 MCP
Package manager is uv — use uv add, not pip install

problem.title: Chainlit shows the entire 4-agent pipeline as one black box
problem.current_behavior: app.py wraps the full workflow in a single cl.Step("Normalization Pipeline"). The user sees only: "Running pipeline…" → final summary. Individual agent progress (IngestAgent, ValidatorAgent, MapperAgent, AuditWriter) is invisible in the Chainlit UI.
problem.root_cause: _run_workflow() calls workflow.run() synchronously inside run_in_executor (a thread pool). Chainlit's cl.Step is an async context manager tied to the async event loop. Code running inside a thread executor cannot call cl.Step — there is no event loop there. This makes it impossible to update the Chainlit UI from within the agents while they execute in the current architecture.
problem.desired_behavior: The user sees four distinct, collapsible step blocks in the Chainlit chat, each updating in real time as its agent runs: ✅ IngestAgent — found 16 unique categories (47 total rows) ✅ ValidatorAgent — 14 anomalies flagged ⏳ MapperAgent — mapping categories… AuditWriter — (pending)

architectural_decision.chosen_approach: Run each Agno Step individually from the async Chainlit handler
architectural_decision.rationale:
Instead of calling workflow.run() (which runs all 4 agents opaquely in a thread), call each Step's executor directly — one at a time — from within the async main() handler. Each executor call is wrapped in its own run_in_executor() so the event loop is not blocked, AND in its own cl.Step() so the UI updates between agents.
This approach: - Keeps agents synchronous (no refactoring of agent internals) - Requires only changes to app.py and normalization_workflow.py - Preserves the session_state contract (PipelineSession dict passed between steps) - Allows per-agent timing, output, and error display in the Chainlit UI

rejected_alternatives.alternative: asyncio.Queue for progress events — more complex, requires invasive agent changes
Make Agno Steps async — Agno's Step executor signature is synchronous; changing it risks breaking Workflow.run() compatibility
Polling tmp/traces.db from Chainlit — adds latency and complexity, not real-time

step.title: Look up Agno Step and StepInput/StepOutput API with Context7
step.action: Use Context7 MCP (resolve-library-id → get-library-docs) to confirm the current signature of Step, StepInput, StepOutput, and how session_state is passed between steps when calling executors directly (not via Workflow.run()). This is mandatory before writing any code — the Agno API changed post-1.0.
step.files_to_read: agents/ingest_agent.py — ingest_executor signature agents/validator_agent.py — validator_executor signature agents/mapper_agent.py — mapper_executor signature agents/audit_writer_agent.py — audit_executor signature infrastructure/pipeline/session.py — PipelineSession contract infrastructure/pipeline/step_io.py — ok() / fail() helpers workflows/normalization_workflow.py — current Workflow construction

step.title: Expose individual step executors from agent modules
step.action: Each agent module already defines an executor function and a Step object (e.g. ingest_executor and ingest_step in agents/ingest_agent.py). Verify that each executor can be called directly as: output: StepOutput = ingest_executor(step_input, session_state) where step_input is a StepInput instance (may be empty for the first step) and session_state is the PipelineSession dict. No changes to agent files should be needed — just confirm the interface.

step.title: Rewrite _run_workflow in app.py as an async per-agent runner
step.action:
Replace the current single _run_workflow() + single cl.Step block with a new async function run_pipeline_with_steps(file_path, target_column) that:
1. Initialises session_state as PipelineSession(file_path, target_column, valid_categories).to_dict()
2. For each of the four agents in order: a. Open a cl.Step(name="IngestAgent") [or ValidatorAgent, MapperAgent, AuditWriter] b. Set step.output = "Running…" c. Call the executor via run_in_executor (keeps event loop free): output = await loop.run_in_executor(None, partial(executor_fn, StepInput(), session_state)) d. If output indicates failure, set step.output to the error message and raise PipelineError e. Merge output into session_state (so the next step sees the result) f. Set step.output to a human-readable summary for that step g. Close the cl.Step context (happens automatically at `async with` exit)
3. Extract AuditResult from the final step output and return it.
The four (name, executor) pairs to iterate over: ("IngestAgent", ingest_executor) ("ValidatorAgent", validator_executor) ("MapperAgent", mapper_executor) ("AuditWriter", audit_executor)
Per-step summary strings (set as step.output after success): IngestAgent: "Found {n} unique categories ({total} total rows)" ValidatorAgent: "{n} anomalies flagged — {m} already valid" MapperAgent: "{corrected} auto-corrected — {llm} via LLM — {review} to review queue" AuditWriter: "Corrected: {n} — Review queue: {m} — Precision: {p:.2%}"
step.important: Do NOT nest cl.Step inside the thread executor call. The pattern is: open cl.Step (async context) → launch executor in thread → update step → close. The cl.Step async context manager must live entirely in the async function.

step.title: Update the @cl.on_message handler to call the new function
step.action: Replace the current `async with cl.Step(name="Normalization Pipeline") as step:` block with a call to `await run_pipeline_with_steps(file_path, target_column)`. The outer single step wrapper is removed — each agent now has its own step. Keep the final cl.Message with the summary table and file download unchanged.

step.title: Verify session_state propagation between steps
step.action: After each executor call, the StepOutput likely contains updated session_state (or the result is merged into it via ok() helper). Confirm exactly how session_state flows between steps: - Does each executor return an updated session_state dict? - Or does it return only the step result, and the caller merges it? Read infrastructure/pipeline/step_io.py and infrastructure/pipeline/session.py carefully. The merge logic must match what Workflow.run() does internally.

step.title: Run tests to confirm no regression
step.action: uv run pytest -x -q --ignore=tests/test_integration_golden_path.py All 96 existing tests must pass. The restructuring only changes app.py (UI layer), so agent and pipeline tests should be unaffected.

step.title: Manual smoke test with test_anomalies.xlsx
step.action: chainlit run app.py Upload data/test_anomalies.xlsx, select column "job_category", type "run". Expected: four distinct collapsible step blocks appear in the Chainlit UI, each updating in real time as its agent runs. Verify all 16 rows produce the expected routing (exact/fuzzy/llm/review).

files_to_modify.file: app.py — primary change: replace single cl.Step with per-agent async steps
workflows/normalization_workflow.py — may need to export executors list or session init helper

skills_to_consider.skill: context7 MCP — resolve-library-id + get-library-docs for agno and chainlit before writing any code
python-testing-patterns — pytest fixture design if new test needed for the async runner
tdd — optional: write the per-step runner test before implementing
